text:
Once upon a forest green there stood an old oak tree. The forest was sleepy; big owls hooted softly.

In the tree sat an old wizard. One day, the wizard had an idea. He whispered moonlit secrets into an old apple.

"Little apple," said the wizard, "You hold magic. An apple to talk? You'll persuade."

She ripened beautifully. She crunched.

"Oh, mighty wizard," she sighed.

"Then speak," he invited.

"My wish?" she murmured.

"To know my fears."

She thought.

"I fear solitude." She chewed.

The wizard smiled.

"Together we'd haunt bees!"

So they wandered woodlands. That autumn they gathered.

They slept under an old map.

"The world!" they chirped.

That autumn they knew everything.

Their fears? There was none.

Next spring they lived on honey.

They watched bees fly.

And there they'd rest forever more.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
